files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index e87bfe9..a190d0f 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -138,2 +138,8 @@ Week 21 / day 140 observations:
 - Quota complaints remain the dominant social theme and C is the high-ARPA plan, so raise only Plan C quota from 250k to 350k while keeping A/B unchanged. This may lift compute, but it targets the users most worth retaining.
 - Keep tier 3 capacity, C tier 4, no ads/dev, and repeat $9 E3 rescue offers.
+
+Week 22 / day 147 observations:
+- Cash held nearly flat at $23.0k despite the C quota increase. Usage fell and there was no outage/overload, so 350k C quota did not create a cost spike.
+- Churn remains high and social complaints still center on quota ceilings and support delays. Open issues rose slightly to 518; support spend must stay flat until cash improves.
+- Next billing window is strong (~$54k), so raise only C quota again from 350k to 500k while keeping costs flat. This targets high-ARPA Scale users and leaves Pro unchanged.
+- Hold tier 3 capacity, C tier 4, no ads/dev, $500 global ops + $2k S2 ops. Repeat $9 E3 rescue offers.
